cognates.gloss: king (Negus), title of the Abyssinian monarch
cognates.correspondence_path: Am ን-ג-ś ↔ Ar ن-ج-ش — clear lexical correspondence
cognates.notes: The root n-g-ś is specific to Ethio-Semitic (Ge'ez/Amharic) and is often debated as a loanword into or from other Cushitic or Semitic contacts.

cognates.correspondence_path: Am ን-ג-ሥ ↔ He נ-ג-ש — regular consonant reflexes but semantic shift from 'king' to 'oppressor/driver'
cognates.notes: The semantic connection between 'king/ruler' and 'oppressor' is recognized in various Semitic cognates.

caveats: v0 prototype: this is an LLM-generated analysis, not a lexicon lookup. Results may be incomplete or incorrect, especially for Syriac, Tigrinya, and rare forms. Verify against Wiktionary or a scholarly lexicon before citing.

The root n-g-ś is distinctively Ethio-Semitic; while the consonants appear to align with North-West Semitic n-g-š, some scholars argue it is an ancient loan into Ge'ez rather than a pure cognate of the Hebrew 'oppress' root.
